metadata.description: Research intelligence topics for Changeis Inc. Drives automated monitoring, daily briefs, and opportunity tracking.

changes: Updated T01 (FAA ATEPS-II): Added Tactical Intelligence Platform (693KA9-ITSP-001) as new FAA Office of Investigations sources sought; ATEPS-II ODRA docket static 13+ months (ODRA-25-965 remains most recent); Changeis seat 693KA925D00004 ($114.7M) uncontested; added 'Tactical Intelligence Platform' and 'FAA ODRA decisions' to keywords

Updated T02 (FAA NAS Modernization): Added ATLAS ChBA Phase 2 announcement (noted in weekly digest Apr 13-16); added Remote Tower Systems solicitation (693KA8-26-T-00016, deadline Jun 22 2026); added AESE RFI (693KA8-26-RFI-AESE, deadline May 12 2026); added 'ATLAS ChBA' and 'Remote Tower Systems' to keywords

Updated T06 (Federal AI Policy): GSA GSAR 552.239-7001 confirmed excluded from MAS Refresh 31 — now targeting Refresh 32 (spring/summer 2026); MAS Refresh 31 (Mass Mod A914) released April 2, 2026 — TDR mandatory for ALL MAS SINs, 60-day acceptance window ~Jun 2, 2026; OMB M-26-10 signed — CIOs must report all IT contracts monthly starting May 2026, sunsets Oct 2026; added 'MAS Refresh 31', 'TDR mandatory MAS', 'OMB M-26-10' to keywords

Updated T07 (DOGE Impact): FY27 budget proposes 10% ($73B) cuts to non-defense civilian agencies; CISA, NIST, IRS hardest hit; White House IT topline set at $75.7B (lower than FY26); OPM procurement operations fully halted after layoffs; added 'FY27 civilian budget cuts', 'OPM procurement halted' to keywords

Updated T09 (Government AI/ML): GPT-5.5 live (OpenAI, Apr 23-26 2026) with agentic workspace integration across Slack/Gmail; Google rebrand Vertex AI → Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (cryptographic agent IDs, anomaly detection, 200+ models); Gartner first-ever AI agent report: 42% of companies plan agent deployment within 12 months, 40%+ projects predicted to fail by end-2027 due to governance gaps; Anthropic holds 40% of enterprise LLM API spend (up from <10% in 2023); added 'GPT-5.5', 'Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform', 'Gartner AI agent deployment', 'federal agent governance' to keywords

Updated T10 (GSA OASIS+): MAS Refresh 31 TDR mandate is direct impact — Changeis must accept Mass Mod A914 by ~Jun 2, 2026; OASIS+ Symphony Portal SMS MFA ending Apr 30 2026 — must switch to authenticator app; OASIS+ Phase II rolling awards expected Q3 FY2026; 120+ new Q&As posted Apr 15 2026; added 'MAS Refresh 31 TDR', 'OASIS+ Phase II awards', 'Symphony portal MFA' to keywords

Updated T12 (SAM.gov Monitoring): OMB M-26-10 increases CIO oversight of IT contracting — expect slower award cycles and greater scrutiny; FY27 10% civilian cuts will drive IDIQ/GWAC vehicle consolidation; updated notes

Updated T14 (Competitive Intel): SSA CGI Federal $197.7M ITSSC task order (Mar 2026); Leidos/CACI DHS desktop support $18.9M award; CMS Goldman Edwards-Tantus $101.6M (CCSQ SIO) and Lewin Group $121.5M (CMMI) awards; Booz Allen FBI IT Learning Support $7.7M; added 'CGI Federal SSA', 'Goldman Edwards-Tantus CMS' to keywords

Updated T17 (Army MAPS): Proposal deadline May 1, 2026 — CRITICAL ACTION REQUIRED. All QPs must use May 1 2023 recency anchor per Amendment 01. CUI via DoD Safe. This entry will be retired post-award (Sep 2026) or converted to task order monitoring topic

Added T18 (Civilian Agency AI Procurement — DOT/FAA/DHS): New topic for civilian agency AI procurement driven by DOJ AI analytics RFI, HUD Next Gen Transformation, CMS IT awards, Gartner 42% agent deployment data, GSAR AI clause impact

Added T19 (Federal Agent Governance and AI Risk): New topic for federal agent deployment governance, driven by Gartner data, governance gap predictions, and Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform launch — directly relevant to Changeis AI advisory positioning
Updated metadata: version 1.2.0 → 1.3.0 (structural — 2 new topics added)

Updated T01 (FAA ATEPS-II): Added ASRC Federal $437M SLES award (Apr 7, 2026) — competitive FAA NAS engineering services vehicle to monitor for future ATEPS-adjacent task orders; added context on FAA Peraton BNATCS $1.5B contract (prime integrator) and $19-20B supplemental request from Sec. Duffy; noted HigherGov listing shows SAIC ATEPS-II Screening Information Request (SIR-7) posted with Apr 16 2026 deadline — early indicator of upcoming task order competition

Updated T02 (FAA NAS Modernization): Brand New Air Traffic Control System (BNATCS) — Peraton confirmed as $1.5B prime integrator (contract value disclosed Feb 10 2026); radar replacement installations beginning Q2 2026 (612 radars by Jun 2028, $1.1B RTX/Indra); CAP (Common Automation Platform) next-gen ATC system under development; DOT Secretary Duffy seeking additional $19-20B for full overhaul

Updated T03 (DHS CISA Recompetes): CISA IDIQ RFP now expected mid-2026 (slipped from Feb 2026 projection); added new recompete: CISA Enterprise Engineering & Operations Support Services (CEEOSS) — Sev1Tech incumbent, GSA Schedule, award Q3 FY2026, sol. released Feb 2026; DEFEND task order extensions confirmed through April 30, 2027

Updated T04 (DHS Component IT): No new material changes week of Apr 14-20; CBP C4I solicitation released Mar 25 per schedule; CBP Tech Service Desk sol. tracking for ~May 2026; added note on ICE SEVP IT/PMO (6 bidders, $35.3M) as lower-competition opportunity

Updated T06 (Federal AI Policy): GSA GSAR 552.239-7001 comment period closed Apr 3 2026 — awaiting final rule for Refresh 32; White House National AI Framework (Mar 20 2026) companion legislative recommendations released; EO signed Mar 26 2026 on DEI requiring new clause in all contracts by Apr 25 2026 (tangential compliance impact); Bloomberg Gov reports FY2026 federal consulting market at $18.7B — rebounding from DOGE cuts

Updated T07 (DOGE Impact): Bloomberg Government data (Apr 13 2026): FY2026 civilian professional services market at $18.7B, within 0.5% of FY2025 levels — market rebounding; DOGE-related 8(a) contract reviews (Wave 3, Jan 2026) compliance audits completed Feb 28 2026 — monitoring for enforcement actions; NDAA Jun 2026 cost/pricing data threshold rising from $2.5M to $10M; total estimated DOGE savings claimed: $215B (disputed)

Updated T08 (Federal Cloud/Zero Trust): NEW: CISA issued binding operational directive requiring all federal civilian agencies to implement zero trust architecture by Sep 30, 2026 — accelerated from prior timeline by 6 months in response to government cyberattacks; NSA Zero Trust Implementation Guideline Primer (Jan 2026) formalizes 5-phase ZT framework; DoD ZT FY2027 deadline unchanged

Updated T10 (GSA OASIS+/Contract Vehicles): NEW: GSA Alliant 3 GWAC Phase 1 awards issued Feb 20 2026 (43 awardees including Booz Allen, SAIC, Peraton, ECS, CACI, Leidos, Deloitte, GDIT, BAE Systems, Accenture, ManTech, Serco, Empower AI, Sev1Tech); Notice to Proceed issued Mar 10 2026 — Alliant 3 is now live for ordering through Mar 2031; OASIS+ Phase 2 continuous enrollment remains open (no close date); FAQ doc updated Apr 15 2026; SMS auth for Symphony Portal ending Apr 30 2026

Updated T11 (CMMC 2.0): Corrected Phase 2 hard deadline — Nov 10 2026 (not Oct 31 2026) is when mandatory Level 2 C3PAO certification becomes required for applicable new contracts; Oct 31 2026 figure was widely circulated but Nov 10 is the accurate 48 CFR rule anniversary; DoD FY2027 ZT deadline aligns with Phase 3

Updated T17 (Army MAPS): Amendment 01 released April 14 2026 — KEY CHANGES: (1) QP recency window anchored to May 1 2026 — only projects with performance completed on/after May 1 2023 qualify (firms must re-check QPs); (2) Passthrough rate scoring now applies to both prime and subcontractor QPs; (3) CUI supporting documents now require separate submission via DoD Safe (not CHESS); (4) Cover letter updated with new CUI identification section. Proposals due May 1 2026 still — NO EXTENSION. Last updated Apr 16 on SAM.gov.

Updated T14 (Competitive Intel): Alliant 3 Phase 1 awardees confirmed — Booz Allen, SAIC, Peraton, ECS Federal, CACI, Deloitte, GDIT, BAE Systems, Accenture Federal, Leidos, ManTech, Serco, Empower AI, Sev1Tech, Agile Defense, Guidehouse, KBR Wyle, others (43 total). Booz Allen projecting low-double-digit civilian revenue contraction FY2026 — teaming window open. DOGE Wave 3 (Jan 2026) 8(a) pass-through reviews could create incumbent vulnerability on small-business vehicles.
Updated metadata: version 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0, last_reviewed updated to 2026-04-20, next_review updated to 2026-04-27
Updated T03 (DHS CISA Recompetes): Added $18-20B CISA IDIQ successor vehicle details; updated PDNS value from $128.5M to $105M (corrected); added CDM-DEFEND successor contract tracking

Updated T04 (DHS Enterprise IT): Expanded to include CBP C5I C4I recompete ($50-100M), CBP Cyber Security Operations Support recompete ($100M+), ICE ITSS recompete ($201M), ICE Enterprise Support Services ($63.9M)
Added T15 (TSA IT and Operations): New critical topic for TSA NEXUS ($100M+), TEAS ($50-100M), FOCUS ($100M+) contracts all due Q2-Q3 FY2026
Added T16 (USCG Cybersecurity and C5I): New high priority topic for Coast Guard Cyber Command IOM contract and CG-C5I sustainment opportunities

Updated T07 (DOGE Impact): Refined notes — DOGE confirmed sunset July 4, 2026; 10,000+ contracts terminated; fixed-price and cost-justification requirements persist; IT consulting >$10M requires DOGE approval still
Updated T08 (Federal Cloud/Zero Trust): Added CISA IDIQ as Zero Trust delivery vehicle
Updated T10 (GSA MAS): OASIS legacy ended; OASIS+ Phase 2 open enrollment as of Jan 2026 — no formal deadline; updated as key vehicle
Updated T13 (FAR Overhaul/RFO): Corrected framing — FAR Part 12 commercial-first, not Part 10; added CSO and OTA references
Updated T14 (Competitive Intel): Added Synergy as USCG incumbent; noted CACI as CBP C4I incumbent; Leidos as CBP Cyber incumbent
Updated Army MAPS: T17 added — RFP officially issued April 6, 2026 (confirmed live); offers due May 1, 2026; awards September 2026
Updated T06 (Federal AI Policy): Added White House National AI Framework (March 20, 2026); OPM AI Workforce programs; TRUMP AMERICA AI Act
Removed T11 (CMMC 2.0): Lowered priority — frequency changed to monthly, notes updated with Oct 31 2026 hard gate; retained but de-prioritized
Updated metadata: version 1.0.0 -> 1.1.0, next_review updated

topics.name: FAA ATEPS-II Task Orders
topics.notes: IDIQ ceiling: Noblis $709.1M (awarded Dec 2024), SAIC $629.8M (awarded Jul 2025). Changeis seat: 693KA925D00004 ($114.7M). Task order competition active battleground through 2035. SETIS contract also active for additional FAA engineering task orders. NEW (Apr 7 2026): ASRC Federal awarded $437M Second Level Engineering Services (SLES) contract — 5-year IDIQ for 24/7 field support, NAS maintenance, systems integration. NEW (Apr 27 2026): Tactical Intelligence Platform sources sought (693KA9-ITSP-001, NAICS 541990) posted — FAA Office of Investigations & Professional Responsibility seeking travel security intelligence platform; deadline May 15, 2026; contact Stephania Ivory at stephania.l.ivory@faa.gov. ODRA WATCH: FAA ODRA docket static since ODRA-25-965 (March 21, 2025) — 13+ months of silence; no protests against Changeis ATEPS-II seat; seat remains uncontested. Multiple eFAST recompetes active in 2026. Primary teaming opportunity for Changeis.

topics.name: FAA NAS Modernization
topics.notes: Broader FAA air traffic organization spend beyond ATEPS-II. BNATCS: Peraton $1.5B prime integrator (Feb 10 2026); 612 radar replacements by Jun 2028 ($1.1B, RTX/Indra). DOT Sec. Duffy seeking additional $19-20B supplemental funding. CAP (Common Automation Platform) in development. NEW (Apr 2026): ATLAS ChBA (Contract-Based Approach) Phase 2 announced — monitor for task order competition. NEW (Apr 27 2026): Remote Tower Systems solicitation (693KA8-26-T-00016) active, deadline Jun 22 2026 — subcontracting opportunity. FAA Alternative Energy Systems Equipment (AESE) RFI (693KA8-26-RFI-AESE) posted Apr 22 2026, deadline May 12 2026 — NAICS 335999. HUM MALSR Demo and Replacement at Houma-Terrebonne Airport (NAICS 237310) posted Apr 23. Peraton subcontracting pipeline significant. Emerging areas: UAM/AAM integration, UAS UTM, autonomous flight.

topics.name: DHS CISA Recompetes and New IDIQ Vehicle
topics.notes: Three parallel tracks: (1) ~24 FY2026 CISA task order recompetes including $321M DEFEND F (7 bidders), $271M Program Mgmt Support (3 bidders), $105M PDNS (6 bidders), $20M Integrated Ops Div, $12.6M PERT Support. (2) Major new vehicle: $18-20B CISA IDIQ (GSA-assisted), covering 5 service areas + products, replaces CDM DEFEND structure; RFP originally projected Feb 2026 — now slipped to mid-2026 (Q2-Q3), award Feb 2027. All DEFEND task orders extended to April 30, 2027. (3) CISA Enterprise Engineering & Operations Support Services (CEEOSS) — Sev1Tech incumbent, GSA Schedule, Labor Hour, award Q3 FY2026, solicitation released Feb 2026 — covers modern IT infrastructure, emerging technologies, hybrid cloud, mobility/collaboration for CISA OCIO. NOTE (Apr 2026): FY27 budget proposes cuts to CISA — monitor for program scope changes. Small business set-asides expected in SA5 and PA1 of new IDIQ. High BD priority — pursue teaming NOW.

topics.name: DHS Component IT Recompetes — CBP, ICE, FLETC
topics.notes: Active recompetes: CBP C4I Systems (CACI incumbent, $50-100M, Alliant 2, award Q4 FY2026, solicitation released Mar 25, 2026 per schedule); CBP Cyber Security Ops Support (Leidos incumbent, $100M+, award Q2-Q3 FY2026); CBP Tech Service Desk (Patriot LLC incumbent, $100M+, STARS III/8a, sol. ~May 2026). ICE recompetes: $201M ITSS (12 bidders), $63.9M Enterprise Support Services (14 bidders), $35.3M SEVP IT/PMO (6 bidders — lower competition opportunity), $10.1M Internet Research & Data Analytics (3 bidders — lowest competition). NEW (Apr 23 2026): DHS OPO Desktop Support 3.0 awarded to CACI NSS ($18.9M) — ongoing DHS IT services demand confirmed. UPDATE: CBP C4I on track per Mar 25 sol. release. $191B+ DHS funding from One Big Beautiful Bill creates expanded pipeline.

topics.name: FMCSA Motus System Support
topics.notes: Phase 1 (supporting companies) live December 2025 at motus.dot.gov. Phase 2 (all users: carriers, brokers, freight forwarders) rolling out through 2026. ACTIVE BLOCKER (Apr 27 2026): IST-1029 cron job update blocked — need finalized letter disable list from Motus SWAT team (only partial list from Abhi received). CDLIS 26.3.0 production deployment pushed to 04/30 due to IAM permission issue. FCWD next planned release 05/07. Monitor for IT support, integration, and enhancement opportunities as platform scales. Multi-year O&M and enhancement opportunity.

topics.name: Federal AI Policy, Governance, and Workforce
topics.notes: GSAR 552.239-7001 confirmed excluded from MAS Refresh 31 — targeting Refresh 32 (spring/summer 2026) — will impose 'American AI Systems' mandate, 72-hour incident reporting, data rights provisions, and 'Unbiased AI Principles'. NEW (Apr 2 2026): MAS Refresh 31 (Mass Mod A914) released — TDR (Transactional Data Reporting) becomes mandatory for ALL MAS SINs; existing MAS contractors must accept within 60 days (~by Jun 2, 2026); Price Reductions Clause ending; TDR reporting begins Jul 1 2026. Changeis MAS contract directly impacted — accept Mass Mod ASAP. NEW (Apr 2026): OMB M-26-10 — CIO must report monthly on all IT contracts, collect vendor pricing/utilization data, share with OMB/GSA; policy starts May 2026, sunsets Oct 2026 — expect slower award cycles and greater cost scrutiny. White House National Policy Framework for AI (March 20 2026). EO 14365 (Dec 2025). OMB M-25-21/M-25-22 (Apr 3 2025) — primary AI governance memos. Cleared AI professional talent gap acute.

topics.name: DOGE Contracting Impact and Post-DOGE Landscape
topics.notes: DOGE sunsets July 4, 2026 (functions absorbing into OMB/OPM permanently). Over 10,000 contracts terminated/descoped. Market rebounding: Bloomberg Government FY2026 civilian consulting at $18.7B — within 0.5% of FY2025. NEW (Apr 2026): Trump FY27 budget proposes 10% ($73B) cuts to non-defense civilian agencies — CISA, NIST, IRS hardest hit; White House IT topline $75.7B (lower than FY26). Procurement consolidation toward IDIQ/GWAC vehicles expected. NEW: OPM procurement operations fully halted after layoffs — expect service gaps and delayed solicitations. IT consulting contracts >$10M still require DOGE approval. NDAA Jun 2026: cost/pricing data threshold rising $2.5M → $10M. Wave 3 (Jan 2026): DoD 8(a) contracts >$20M pass-through compliance audit — enforcement monitoring continues. Monitor for re-solicitation of terminated contracts.

topics.name: Federal Cloud Migration and Zero Trust
topics.notes: CRITICAL (Apr 17 2026): CISA binding operational directive — ALL federal civilian agencies must implement zero trust architecture by September 30, 2026 (accelerated 6 months); $2.1B emergency funding allocated. NSA Zero Trust Implementation Guideline Primer (Jan 2026) formalizes 5-phase ZT framework. DoD FY2027 ZT deadline unchanged. JWCC Next active. Air Force Cloud One $88M Oracle task order Feb 2026. New CISA $18-20B IDIQ includes ZT and CDM capabilities as core service areas. FCEB agencies under intense pressure to meet Sep 30 2026 ZT milestone — expect surge in ZT implementation task orders Q2-Q3 FY2026. NOTE: FY27 budget pressures may constrain cloud migration spend for some civilian agencies.

topics.name: Government AI/ML Implementation
topics.notes: Agencies transitioning AI pilots to full production through 2026. CFO Act agencies required to publish AI strategies by Oct 2025 (done). NEW (Apr 23-26 2026): GPT-5.5 launched with agentic workspace integration across Slack/Gmail — autonomous execution now a paid-plan feature, not prototype. Google rebranded Vertex AI → Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (cryptographic agent IDs, 200+ models, anomaly detection) at Google Cloud Next. NEW (Apr 7 2026): Gartner first-ever AI agent report — 42% of companies plan agent deployment within 12 months; 40%+ projects predicted to fail by end-2027 due to governance gaps (not technology). Anthropic holds 40% of enterprise LLM API spend (up from <10% in 2023); OpenAI at 27% (down from 50%). Stanford 2026 AI Index: AI agents jumped from 12% to 66% success on real computer tasks. GSAR 552.239-7001 'American AI Systems' mandate pending in Refresh 32 — will reshape AI procurement across all GSA schedules. Cleared AI professional talent gap is acute — Changeis staffing + delivery positioning opportunity.

topics.name: GSA OASIS+ and Contract Vehicles
topics.notes: IMMEDIATE ACTION — Symphony MFA: SMS authentication ending Apr 30 2026 — all OSP users must switch to authenticator app by Apr 29 (go to Profile → My Credentials tab). IMMEDIATE ACTION — MAS Refresh 31: Mass Mod A914 (TDR mandate) released Apr 2 2026 — Changeis MAS contract must accept within 60 days (~Jun 2, 2026); TDR reporting begins Jul 1 2026; Price Reductions Clause ends. Contact maspmo@gsa.gov with questions. OASIS+ Phase II: 120+ new Q&As posted Apr 15 2026; rolling awards expected Q3 FY2026 (Jul-Sep); continuously open across all 6 solicitations. Alliant 3 GWAC: Phase 1 live for ordering (43 awardees, Mar 10 2026 NTP, expires Mar 2031). STARS III active for 8(a) CBP task orders. Changeis should pursue OASIS+ on-ramp if not already on vehicle.

topics.name: CMMC 2.0 Compliance and DoD Cybersecurity
topics.notes: CMMC Level 1/2 self-attestations now in DoD solicitations since Phase 1 (Nov 10, 2025). Phase 2 hard gate: November 10, 2026 — mandatory Level 2 C3PAO third-party certification required for applicable new contracts. Phase 3 (Nov 10 2027): L2 required for option exercises; L3 DIBCAC requirements introduced. Phase 4 (Nov 10 2028): Full implementation. 8(a) set-aside DoD contracts >$20M subject to pass-through compliance audit (Jan 2026 Wave 3). C3PAO assessment backlog is real — Changeis should confirm compliance posture and schedule assessment NOW to beat queue before Nov 2026 deadline. Army MAPS requires CMMC Level 2 self-attestation — verify before May 1 proposal submission.

topics.name: SAM.gov Opportunity Monitoring — Core NAICS
topics.notes: Broad daily sweep across Changeis core NAICS. Feeds VM2 daily brief. Separate from named-program monitoring. Priority filter: DHS, DOT, Army, GSA agencies. DOGE contract re-solicitations may create short-fuse opportunities. UPDATE (Apr 2026): OMB M-26-10 increases CIO oversight of IT contracting — expect slower award cycles and greater cost scrutiny starting May 2026. FY27 10% civilian cuts will drive procurement consolidation toward IDIQ/GWAC vehicles — adjust monitoring emphasis accordingly. Bloomberg Gov: civilian consulting market rebounding to FY2025 levels — increased solicitation volume expected Q2-Q3 FY2026.

topics.name: Revolutionary FAR Overhaul (RFO) and Acquisition Reform
topics.notes: EO 14265 targets defense acquisition modernization. FAR Part 12 commercial-first mandate — agencies must prove commercial solution cannot meet need before custom dev. CSO and OTA pathways expanding. Changes Changeis proposal strategy: lead with commercial-off-the-shelf solutions first, custom second. Fixed-price contracting preference reinforced. NDAA Jun 2026: cost/pricing data certified cost threshold rising from $2.5M to $10M — reduces reporting burden on mid-size contracts. Draft DFARS revisions expected 2026 per EO 14265 mandate. OMB M-26-10 (Apr 2026) layered on top — monthly CIO reporting requirement adds administrative oversight to IT contracting.

topics.name: Competitive Intelligence — Large Integrators and Incumbents
topics.notes: Key incumbents to track: Noblis (FAA ATEPS-II $709M), SAIC (FAA ATEPS-II $629M), CACI (CBP C4I C5I; DHS Desktop Support 3.0 $18.9M award Apr 23 2026), Leidos (CBP Cyber Security Ops $100M+), Synergy (USCG CG-C5I $32M task order Mar 2026), Booz Allen (2x CDM DEFEND groups; FBI IT Learning Support $7.7M Apr 2026), Sev1Tech (CISA CEEOSS incumbent), CGI Federal (SSA ITSSC $197.7M task order Mar 2026), Goldman Edwards-Tantus (CMS CCSQ SIO $101.6M Apr 2026). NEW intel: CMS also awarded Lewin Group $121.5M for CMMI — CMS is hot FedCiv target agency. DOJ ADS awarded $1.3M data analytics task Apr 2026 — signals DOJ buying in AI analytics space now. Alliant 3 Phase 1 (43 awardees) — subcontracting under Alliant 3 is a Changeis pathway. DOGE Wave 3 8(a) pass-through audits may create incumbent vulnerability.

topics.name: TSA IT Contracts — NEXUS, TEAS, FOCUS
topics.notes: Three active TSA IT procurements all targeting Q2-Q3 FY2026 award: (1) NEXUS — IT Ops & Maintenance, >$100M, FFP BPA, performance through May 2031, Springfield VA — broad O&M scope including cloud, AI, data analytics, telecom; (2) TEAS — Enterprise Engineering & Architecture, $50-100M, FFP BPA, award Q2 FY2026, May 2031 completion — new tech integration and design; (3) FOCUS (F2026072648) — Field Ops & Customer Support, >$100M, FFP task order, award Q3 FY2026, SPOC helpdesk + field IT specialist support for ~85,000 TSA personnel at ~500 sites — STRONG-rated in Apr 25 daily brief. ADDITIONAL (Week of Apr 20-21 deep dives): TSA ATSS (F2025071485, $100M+, 541330 STRONG-rated), TSA OTSS (541330-1485), TSA Dell H/W S/W O&M (F2025071609), TSA Kronos/UKG (F2025071541), TSA Okta (F2025071410), TSA WebEOC (F2025070231), TSA Training Management System/ACADIS (F2025071720) — multiple additional TSA work streams entering final solicitation phase. Monitor SAM.gov for formal solicitation releases on NEXUS and TEAS.

topics.name: USCG Cybersecurity and C5I Digital Transformation
topics.notes: Active opportunity: USCG Cyber Command Integrated Operations Management (IOM) — published March 2026 on DHS APFS, covers CG-CYBER cyberspace operations, NIPRNET/SIPRNET, analytical and technical SME support. Recent award: Synergy $32M task order for CG-C5I sustainment (Mar 2026) — Synergy is likely incumbent to track for teaming. NEW (Apr 20-21 2026 deep dives): CG-C5I BD Services (F2026073065), USCG ACCAS (F2025070621), USCG ALC ISD (F2026072250), USCG Risk Analysis BPA (F2025068857), USCG RCRA Elizabeth City (F2025072195) — multiple USCG work streams active and deep-dived this week. Arctic Security Cutter IT support contracts (11 ASCs under construction, Feb 2026) also monitor. CISA ZT BOD acceleration may generate USCG-specific ZT implementation task orders.

topics.name: Army MAPS — $50B IDIQ
topics.notes: RFP officially issued April 6, 2026 on SAM.gov. PROPOSALS DUE MAY 1, 2026 — NO EXTENSION. $50B IDIQ, 10-year PoP (5-year base + 5-year option). Consolidates RS3 and ITES-3S vehicles. 350 total awards (70 per domain): 5 domains — Engineering/Logistics/Ops, Management/Advisory, RDT&E, Emerging IT, Foundational IT. 30 large business + 25 SB + 15 Commercial-Sector Vendor per domain. AMENDMENT 01 (Apr 14 2026): QP recency anchored to May 1 2026 (only projects completed on/after May 1 2023 qualify); passthrough rate scoring applies to both prime AND subcontractor QPs; CUI docs via DoD Safe (NOT CHESS); cover letter updated with CUI identification section. SAM.gov last updated Apr 16. Gate: Secret facility clearance, ISO 9001:2015, CMMC Level 2 (self), accounting system (LB), CPARS ≤5% marginal. Awards September 2026. POST-AWARD: This topic will convert from BD opportunity to task order monitoring upon award (Sep 2026).
topics.action_required: URGENT: Offers due May 1, 2026 (4 DAYS). Amendment 01 (Apr 14) changed QP recency rules and CUI submission requirements — verify all QPs dated on/after May 1 2023, submit CUI docs via DoD Safe.

topics.name: Civilian Agency AI Procurement — DOT, FAA, DHS, DOJ, HUD, CMS
topics.notes: NEW TOPIC (Apr 27 2026): Civilian agency AI procurement is accelerating across multiple agencies this week. Key signals: (1) DOJ AI-Enabled Scalable Data Analytics Solution RFI (TMF5-1bRFI, NAICS 541512) — active, full and open competition expected; DOJ data analytics adjacent to FBI IT Learning Support ($7.7M Booz Allen Apr 2026); ADS won $1.3M DOJ data analytics task Apr 2026. (2) HUD Next Generation Technology Transformation presolicitation (NOF2026-0005, NAICS 541511, deadline Apr 30 2027 — long runway) — full and open. (3) CMS IT modernization: Goldman Edwards-Tantus $101.6M (CCSQ SIO) and Lewin Group $121.5M (CMMI) awards in Apr 2026 signal major CMS IT spend. (4) GSAR 552.239-7001 AI procurement clause (expected Refresh 32) will require US-only AI tools, government data rights, 72-hr CISA reporting — Changeis AI proposals must be compliance-ready. Gartner: 42% of companies (including federal) plan agent deployment within 12 months. This topic drives keyword monitoring across civilian agency AI solicitations and shapes proposal positioning.

topics.name: Federal Agent Governance and AI Risk Framework
topics.notes: NEW TOPIC (Apr 27 2026): Gartner's first-ever AI agent report (released ~Apr 7 2026, cited at RSAC 2026) confirms 42% of companies plan agent deployment within 12 months — but predicts 40%+ of projects will fail by end-2027 due to governance gaps, not technology. Stanford 2026 AI Index: AI agents jumped from 12% to 66% success on real computer tasks. Google's Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (rebranded from Vertex AI, Apr 23-26 2026) introduces cryptographic agent identities, auditable authorization trails, real-time Agent Anomaly Detection, and Model Armor security controls — this is the architecture enterprise clients (including federal agencies) will be evaluating. Anthropic holds 40% of enterprise LLM API spend (up from <10% in 2023). POSITIONING: Changeis can serve as an informed advisor on federal agent governance — understanding cryptographic agent IDs, anomaly detection, governance frameworks, and GSAR compliance positions Changeis ahead of the market. Monitor NIST AI RMF updates, OMB AI agent guidance, and GSA GSAR clause development for procurement opportunities.
